the equilibrium occurs when growth equals harvest so zero equals point one times p times one minus p over one thousand minus ten minus five sine of pi t over six
solving this quadratic yields a positive root for p that shifts as the sine term varies but the average equilibrium is roughly nine hundred and ten turtles when harvest averages fifteen
climate change reduces carrying capacity linearly so k becomes one thousand minus three t and the new equilibrium equation is zero equals point one times p times one minus p over one thousand minus three t minus ten minus five sine of pi t over six
stability analysis shows that as k shrinks the positive equilibrium root decreases while the negative root remains negative and the population collapses when the discriminant becomes non positive or when the positive root hits zero
the population becomes unsustainable when the maximum possible growth cannot offset the minimum harvest which happens when k drops below the threshold where r times k over four is less than the minimum harvest of five
calculating the critical time reveals that sustainability fails when one thousand minus three t is approximately two hundred and fifty years meaning t is roughly two hundred and fifty years before extinction
